title: How Firm a Foundation
firstLine: How firm a foundation, ye saints of the Lord

authors.name: Anonymous
authors.notes: Published as 'K' in Rippon's Selection of Hymns, 1787.

textAttribution: Text: Anonymous ('K'), in Rippon's Selection of Hymns, 1787.
tuneAttribution: Tune: FOUNDATION, American folk melody, arr. Joseph Funk, 1832.
publicDomainBasis: Anonymous text, published 1787. Tune FOUNDATION is a traditional American folk melody. Both public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
How firm a foundation, ye saints of the Lord,
Is laid for your faith in His excellent word!
What more can He say than to you He hath said,
To you who for refuge to Jesus have fled?

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
"Fear not, I am with thee, O be not dismayed,
For I am thy God and will still give thee aid;
I'll strengthen and help thee, and cause thee to stand
Upheld by My righteous, omnipotent hand."

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
"When through the deep waters I call thee to go,
The rivers of woe shall not thee overflow;
For I will be with thee, thy troubles to bless,
And sanctify to thee thy deepest distress."

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
"When through fiery trials thy pathways shall lie,
My grace, all sufficient, shall be thy supply;
The flame shall not hurt thee; I only design
Thy dross to consume, and thy gold to refine."

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
"The soul that on Jesus has leaned for repose,
I will not, I will not desert to its foes;
That soul, though all hell should endeavor to shake,
I'll never, no, never, no, never forsake!"
